meaning: n.平衡,平静,均衡,保持平衡的能力,沉着,安静
examples: We have achieved an equilibrium in the economy.
He sat down to try and recover his equilibrium.
Any disturbance to the body's state of equilibrium can produce stress.
But the emotional equilibrium for women and work will likely happen woman by woman.
As in all of Mr. Cole's dishes, establishing equilibrium among the various elements is essential.
